title: Platelet membrane-coated drug delivery system
abstract: Disclosed herein is a platelet membrane-coated nanovehicle having an inner core comprising a drug delivery matrix, and an outer shell platelet membrane coating the inner core. The inner core can be any drug delivery matrix capable of delivering a therapeutic agent to a cell. The outer shell platelet membrane can be a natural or synthetic membrane comprising platelet proteins capable of interacting with cancer cells. Also disclosed is a method for treating cancer in a subject that involves administering to the subject a platelet membrane-coated nanovehicle disclosed herein. Also disclosed is a method for treating vascular disease in a subject that involves administering to the subject a platelet membrane-coated nanovehicle disclosed herein.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A platelet membrane-coated nanovehicle, comprising a) a inner core comprising a drug delivery matrix; and b) an outer shell platelet membrane coating the inner core. 2. The platelet membrane-coated nanovehicle of claim 1, wherein the drug delivery matrix comprises an intracellularly active small molecule drug. 3. The platelet membrane-coated nanovehicle of claim 2, wherein the intracellularly active small molecule drug comprises an anti -neoplastic drug. 4. The platelet membrane-coated nanovehicle of claim 3, wherein the anti-neoplastic drug comprises a hydrophobic small molecule. 5. The platelet membrane-coated nanovehicle of claim 4, wherein the anti-neoplastic drug is selected from the group consisting of paclitaxel, camptothecin, and docetaxel. 6. The platelet membrane-coated nanovehicle of claim 3, wherein the anti-neoplastic drug comprises a hydrophilic small molecule. 7. The platelet membrane-coated nanovehicle of claim 6, wherein the anti-neoplastic drug is selected from the group consisting of doxorubicin, cisplatin, and 5-fluorouracil. 8. The platelet membrane-coated nanovehicle of any one of claims 1 to 7, wherein the outer shell platelet membrane comprises endogenous P-selectin protein. 9. The platelet membrane-coated nanovehicle of any one of claims 1 to 8, wherein the outer shell platelet membrane comprises integrin Î±Î¹Î·,Î²3. 10. The platelet membrane-coated nanovehicle of any one of claims 1 to 9, wherein the outer shell platelet membrane comprises a self-recognized immunomodulatory protein selected from the group consisting of CD47, CD55, and CD59. 11. The platelet membrane-coated nanovehicle of any one of claims 1 to 10, wherein the outer shell platelet membrane further comprises a heterologous extracellularly active protein. 12. The platelet membrane-coated nanovehicle of claim 11, wherein the extracellularly active protein comprises tumor necrosis factor (T F)-related apoptosis inducing ligand (TRAIL). 13. The platelet membrane-coated nanovehicle of claim 11, wherein the extracellularly active protein comprises a therapeutic antibody. 14. The platelet membrane-coated nanovehicle of claim 13, wherein the therapeutic antibody comprises cetuximab, trastuzumab, or a combination thereof. 15. The platelet membrane-coated nanovehicle of any one of claims 1 to 14, wherein the drug delivery matrix comprises a polymeric gel. 16. The platelet membrane-coated nanovehicle of any one of claims 1 to 14, wherein the drug delivery matrix comprises an inorganic particle. 17. The platelet membrane-coated nanovehicle of any one of claims 1 to 14, wherein the drug delivery matrix comprises a lipid particle. 18. The platelet membrane-coated nanovehicle of any one of claims 1 to 14, wherein the drug delivery matrix comprises a dendrimer particle. 19. The platelet membrane-coated nanovehicle of any one of claims 1 to 18, wherein the nanovehicle has an average diameter of from lOnm to 1000 nm. 20. The platelet membrane-coated nanovehicle of any one of claims 1 to 18, wherein the nanovehicle has an average zeta potential of from -1 mV to -40 mV. 21. The platelet membrane-coated nanovehicle of any one of claims 1 to 18, wherein the nanovehicle is pH sensitive. 22. A method for treating cancer in a subject, comprising administering to the subject the platelet membrane-coated nanovehicle of any one of claims 1 to 21. 23. The method of claim 22, wherein the platelet is autologous. 24. The method of claim 22 or 23, wherein the cancer overexpresses CD44. 25. The method of any one of claims 22 to 24, wherein the cancer is a circulating cancer cell. 26. The method of any one of claims 22 to 25, wherein the cancer is a metastatic cancer cell. 27. A method for treating vascular disease in a subject, comprising administering to the subject the platelet membrane-coated nanovehicle of claim 1. 28. The method of claim 27, wherein the drug delivery matrix comprises a drug to treat vascular disease. 29. The method of claim 28, wherein the drug comprises heparin to treat blood coagulation. 30. The method of claim 28, wherein the drug comprises doxorubicin to treat coronary restenosis.
